patch.value: GH-248: schema add-type slug-collision overwrite + malformed name validation

pm schema add-type silently overwrites an existing type on case/slug collision and accepts malformed names (spaces, non-identifier chars). Add malformed-name rejection in normalizeAddTypeInput, folder/slug collision detection against different-named existing definitions, and a recase warning when an upsert changes the canonical name's case.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: GH-248 fix: (1) normalizeAddTypeInput now rejects malformed names/aliases via TYPE_NAME_TOKEN_PATTERN (letter-led [A-Za-z0-9_-], no spaces/punctuation); (2) new assertTypeFolderAvailable() rejects distinct type names whose resolved folder slug collides (Spike/Spikes, code-review/code_review, explicit --folder), single-sourcing slug derivation from type-registry.toDefaultFolder (now exported); (3) upsertItemType returns previousName so schema.ts emits a non-blocking type_recased:Old->New warning when a case-variant upsert renames the canonical. Files: core/schema/item-types-file.ts, core/item/type-registry.ts, cli/commands/schema.ts.

patch.value: Added name-token validation, assertTypeFolderAvailable folder-collision guard, and type_recased warning; 100% covered.
add-type rejects spaces/punctuation names and folder collisions; warns on recase
Hardened pm schema add-type: reject malformed type names/aliases, detect folder/slug collisions between distinct types, and warn on case-recase upserts.
GH-248: fix schema add-type silent slug-collision overwrite + malformed-name acceptance
